message.content: does this project have a readme?

1|# noumen 🐍
     2|
     3|The coding agent you `npm install`.
     4|
     5|`noumen` gives you the full agentic coding loop — tool execution, file editing, shell commands, context compaction, and session management — with sandboxed virtual infrastructure that isolates your agent from the host machine.
     6|
     7|Any provider. Any sandbox. One package.
     8|
     9|**[Documentation](https://noumen.dev)** · **[npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/noumen)** · **[GitHub](https://github.com/UpstreetAI/noumen)**
    10|
    11|## Install
    12|
    13|```bash
    14|pnpm add noumen
    15|```
    16|
    17|Then install the provider SDK you need:
    18|
    19|```bash
    20|pnpm add openai           # for OpenAI / OpenRouter / Ollama
    21|pnpm add @anthropic-ai/sdk  # for Anthropic
    22|pnpm add @google/genai      # for Gemini
    23|# Ollama requires no SDK — just install https://ollama.com
    24|```
    25|
    26|## Quick Start
    27|
    28|```typescript
    29|import { Code, LocalSandbox } from "noumen";
    30|import { OpenAIProvider } from "noumen/openai";
    31|
    32|const code = new Code({
    33|  aiProvider: new OpenAIProvider({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY }),
    34|  sandbox: LocalSandbox({ cwd: "/my/project" }),
    35|});
    36|
    37|const thread = code.createThread();
    38|
    39|for await (const event of thread.run("Add a health-check endpoint to server.ts")) {
    40|  switch (event.type) {
    41|    case "text_delta":
    42|      process.stdout.write(event.text);
    43|      break;
    44|    case "tool_use_start":
    45|      console.log(`\n[tool] ${event.toolName}`);
    46|      break;
    47|    case "tool_result":
    48|      console.log(`[result] ${event.result.content.slice(0, 200)}`);
    49|      break;
    50|  }
    51|}
    52|```
    53|
    54|## CLI
    55|
    56|noumen ships a CLI for using the agent directly from the terminal, with any provider.
    57|
    58|```bash
    59|# Interactive mode — auto-detects provider from env vars
    60|npx noumen
    61|
    62|# One-shot with a specific provider
    63|npx noumen -p anthropic "Add error handling to server.ts"
    64|
    65|# Pipe input
    66|cat plan.md | npx noumen -p openai
    67|
    68|# JSONL output for scripting
    69|npx noumen --json -c "List all TODO comments" > events.jsonl
    70|```
    71|
    72|### Setup
    73|
    74|```bash
    75|noumen init
    76|```
    77|
    78|This creates `.noumen/config.json` with your provider and model choice. The CLI also reads `NOUMEN.md` files for project instructions (see [Project Context](#project-context)).
    79|
    80|### Config file
    81|
    82|```json
    83|{
    84|  "provider": "anthropic",
    85|  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
    86|  "permissions": "acceptEdits"
    87|}
    88|```
    89|
    90|Place in `.noumen/config.json` at your project root. The CLI walks up from the working directory to find it.
    91|
    92|### Flags
    93|
    94|| Flag | Description |
    95||------|-------------|
    96|| `-p, --provider` | `openai`, `anthropic`, `gemini`, `openrouter`, `bedrock`, `vertex`, `ollama` |
    97|| `-m, --model` | Model name (provider-specific default if omitted) |
    98|| `--api-key` | Override API key |
    99|| `--base-url` | Override provider base URL |
   100|| `-c, --prompt` | One-shot prompt (non-interactive) |
   101|| `--permission` | Permission mode: `default`, `plan`, `acceptEdits`, `auto`, `bypassPermissions` |
   102|| `--thinking` | Thinking level: `off`, `low`, `medium`, `high` |
   103|| `--max-turns` | Max agent turns before stopping |
   104|| `--json` | Emit JSONL stream events to stdout |
   105|| `--quiet` | Only output final text |
   106|| `--verbose` | Show tool calls and thinking |
   107|| `--cwd` | Working directory |
   108|
   109|### API key resolution
   110|
   111|1. `--api-key` flag
   112|2. Provider-specific env var (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`)
   113|3. `NOUMEN_API_KEY` generic env var
   114|4. `.noumen/config.json` `apiKey` field
   115|
   116|Ollama, Bedrock, and Vertex do not require an API key.
   117|
   118|### Commands
   119|
   120|| Command | Description |
   121||---------|-------------|
   122|| `noumen init` | Create `.noumen/config.json` |
   123|| `noumen sessions` | List past sessions |
   124|| `noumen resume <id>` | Resume a previous session (prefix match) |
   125|
   126|## Providers
   127|
   128|### OpenAI
   129|
   130|```typescript
   131|import { OpenAIProvider } from "noumen/openai";
   132|
   133|const provider = new OpenAIProvider({
   134|  apiKey: "sk-...",
   135|  model: "gpt-4o",      // default
   136|  baseURL: "https://...", // optional, for compatible APIs
   137|});
   138|```
   139|
   140|### Anthropic
   141|
   142|```typescript
   143|import { AnthropicProvider } from "noumen/anthropic";
   144|
   145|const provider = new AnthropicProvider({
   146|  apiKey: "sk-ant-...",
   147|  model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", // default
   148|});
   149|```
   150|
   151|### Google Gemini
   152|
   153|```typescript
   154|import { GeminiProvider } from "noumen/gemini";
   155|
   156|const provider = new GeminiProvider({
   157|  apiKey: "...",                   // Google AI Studio API key
   158|  model: "gemini-2.5-flash",      // default
   159|});
   160|```
   161|
   162|### OpenRouter
   163|
   164|```typescript
   165|import { OpenRouterProvider } from "noumen/openrouter";
   166|
   167|const provider = new OpenRouterProvider({
   168|  apiKey: "sk-or-...",
   169|  model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",  // default
   170|  appName: "My Agent",                 // optional, for openrouter.ai rankings
   171|  appUrl: "https://myapp.com",         // optional
   172|});
   173|```
   174|
   175|### AWS Bedrock (Anthropic)
   176|
   177|Route Anthropic models through AWS Bedrock. Requires `@anthropic-ai/bedrock-sdk`:
   178|
   179|```bash
   180|pnpm add @anthropic-ai/bedrock-sdk
   181|```
   182|
   183|```typescript
   184|import { BedrockAnthropicProvider } from "noumen/bedrock";
   185|
   186|const provider = new BedrockAnthropicProvider({
   187|  region: "us-west-2",                                     // default: us-east-1
   188|  model: "us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0",     // default
   189|  credentials: {                                            // optional, falls back to default chain
   190|    accessKeyId: process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID!,
   191|    secretAccessKey: process.env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY!,
   192|    sessionToken: process.env.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN,
   193|  },
   194|  cacheControl: { enabled: true },                          // optional prompt caching
   195|});
   196|```
   197|
   198|When `credentials` is omitted, the SDK uses the standard AWS credential chain (env vars, `~/.aws/credentials`, IAM roles, etc.).
   199|
   200|### Google Vertex AI (Anthropic)
   201|
   202|Route Anthropic models through Google Cloud Vertex AI. Requires `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` and `google-auth-library`:
   203|
   204|```bash
   205|pnpm add @anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk google-auth-library
   206|```
   207|
   208|```typescript
   209|import { VertexAnthropicProvider } from "noumen/vertex";
   210|
   211|const provider = new VertexAnthropicProvider({
   212|  projectId: "my-gcp-project",
   213|  region: "us-east5",                     // default
   214|  model: "claude-sonnet-4@20250514",      // default
   215|  cacheControl: { enabled: true },        // optional prompt caching
   216|});
   217|```
   218|
   219|When `googleAuth` is omitted, the provider creates a `GoogleAuth` instance using application default credentials. You can pass your own `googleAuth` instance for custom authentication:
   220|
   221|```typescript
   222|import { GoogleAuth } from "google-auth-library";
   223|
   224|const provider = new VertexAnthropicProvider({
   225|  projectId: "my-project",
   226|  googleAuth: new GoogleAuth({ keyFile: "/path/to/service-account.json" }),
   227|});
   228|```
   229|
   230|### Ollama (Local)
   231|
   232|Run models locally with [Ollama](https://ollama.com). No API key needed — just install Ollama and pull a model:
   233|
   234|```bash
   235|ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:32b
   236|ollama serve
   237|```
   238|
   239|```typescript
   240|import { OllamaProvider } from "noumen/ollama";
   241|
   242|const provider = new OllamaProvider({
   243|  model: "qwen2.5-coder:32b",   // default
   244|  baseURL: "http://localhost:11434/v1",  // default
   245|});
   246|```
   247|
   248|The CLI auto-detects a running Ollama server when no cloud API keys are set, so you can simply run `noumen` with Ollama serving in the background.
   249|
   250|## Sandboxes
   251|
   252|A `Sandbox` bundles a `VirtualFs` (filesystem) and `VirtualComputer` (shell execution) into one object. Every file read/write and shell command the agent executes goes through these interfaces — swap the sandbox to control what the agent can access.
   253|
   254|### Local (Node.js) — no isolation
   255|
   256|Backed by `fs/promises` and `child_process`. Use for local development and trusted environments:
   257|
   258|```typescript
   259|import { LocalSandbox } from "noumen";
   260|
   261|const sandbox = LocalSandbox({ cwd: "/my/project" });
   262|```
   263|
   264|### sprites.dev — full sandbox
   265|
   266|Run inside a remote [sprites.dev](https://docs.sprites.dev) container. The agent has no access to the host machine:
   267|
   268|```typescript
   269|import { SpritesSandbox } from "noumen";
   270|
   271|const sandbox = SpritesSandbox({
   272|  token: process.env.SPRITE_TOKEN,
   273|  spriteName: "my-sprite",
   274|});
   275|```
   276|
   277|### Docker — container isolation
   278|
   279|Run the agent inside a Docker container. Requires `dockerode` as an optional peer dependency:
   280|
   281|```bash
   282|pnpm add dockerode
   283|```
   284|
   285|```typescript
   286|import Docker from "dockerode";
   287|import { DockerSandbox } from "noumen";
   288|
   289|const docker = new Docker();
   290|const container = await docker.createContainer({
   291|  Image: "node:22",
   292|  Cmd: ["sleep", "infinity"],
   293|  Tty: false,
   294|});
   295|await container.start();
   296|
   297|const sandbox = DockerSandbox({
   298|  container,
   299|  cwd: "/workspace",
   300|});
   301|
   302|// Use the sandbox normally — all commands/files run inside the container
   303|const code = new Code({ aiProvider, sandbox });
   304|
   305|// Clean up when done
   306|await container.stop();
   307|await container.remove();
   308|```
   309|
   310|You are responsible for container lifecycle (create, start, stop, remove). The sandbox just wraps the running container.
   311|
   312|### E2B — cloud sandbox
   313|
   314|Run the agent inside an [E2B](https://e2b.dev) cloud sandbox. Requires `e2b` as an optional peer dependency:
   315|
   316|```bash
   317|pnpm add e2b
   318|```
   319|
   320|```typescript
   321|import { Sandbox as E2BSandboxSDK } from "e2b";
   322|import { E2BSandbox } from "noumen";
   323|
   324|const e2b = await E2BSandboxSDK.create();
   325|
   326|const sandbox = E2BSandbox({
   327|  sandbox: e2b,
   328|  cwd: "/home/user",
   329|});
   330|
   331|const code = new Code({ aiProvider, sandbox });
   332|
   333|// Clean up when done
   334|await e2b.close();
   335|```
   336|
   337|You are responsible for sandbox lifecycle (create, close). The adapter maps `VirtualFs` and `VirtualComputer` to E2B's `files` and `commands` APIs.
   338|
   339|### Custom sandboxes
   340|
   341|Implement `VirtualFs` and `VirtualComputer` to target any execution environment — Daytona, cloud VMs, or an in-memory test harness. A custom `Sandbox` is any object with `{ fs, computer }`:
   342|
   343|```typescript
   344|import type { Sandbox } from "noumen";
   345|
   346|const sandbox: Sandbox = {
   347|  fs: new MyCustomFs(),
   348|  computer: new MyCustomComputer(),
   349|};
   350|```
   351|
   352|The interfaces are intentionally minimal (one method for shell, eight for filesystem) so adapters are straightforward to write.
   353|
   354|## Options
   355|
   356|```typescript
   357|const code = new Code({
   358|  aiProvider,
   359|  sandbox,
   360|  options: {
   361|    sessionDir: ".noumen/sessions", // JSONL transcript storage path
   362|    model: "gpt-4o",                   // default model
   363|    maxTokens: 8192,                   // max output tokens per turn
   364|    autoCompact: true,                 // auto-compact when context is large
   365|    autoCompactThreshold: 100_000,     // token threshold for auto-compact
   366|    systemPrompt: "...",               // override the built-in system prompt
   367|    cwd: "/working/dir",              // working directory for tools
   368|    skills: [{ name: "...", content: "..." }],
   369|    skillsPaths: [".claude/skills"],   // paths to SKILL.md files on the sandbox filesystem
   370|    projectContext: true,              // load NOUMEN.md / CLAUDE.md from project
   371|
   372|    // Extended thinking / reasoning (see below)
   373|    thinking: { type: "enabled", budgetTokens: 10000 },
   374|
   375|    // Retry / error resilience (see below)
   376|    retry: true,                       // use defaults, or pass a RetryConfig
   377|
   378|    // Cost tracking (see below)
   379|    costTracking: { enabled: true },
   380|  },
   381|});
   382|```
   383|
   384|## Threads
   385|
   386|```typescript
   387|// New thread
   388|const thread = code.createThread();
   389|
   390|// Resume an existing session
   391|const thread = code.createThread({ sessionId: "abc-123", resume: true });
   392|
   393|// Run a prompt (returns an async iterable of stream events)
   394|for await (const event of thread.run("Fix the failing test")) {
   395|  // handle events
   396|}
   397|
   398|// Get conversation history
   399|const messages = await thread.getMessages();
   400|
   401|// Manually compact the conversation
   402|await thread.compact();
   403|
   404|// Abort a running request
   405|thread.abort();
   406|```
   407|
   408|## Stream Events
   409|
   410|| Event | Fields | Description |
   411||-------|--------|-------------|
   412|| `text_delta` | `text` | Incremental text from the model |
   413|| `thinking_delta` | `text` | Incremental thinking/reasoning text from the model |
   414|| `tool_use_start` | `toolName`, `toolUseId` | Model is calling a tool |
   415|| `tool_use_delta` | `input` | Incremental tool call arguments |
   416|| `tool_result` | `toolUseId`, `toolName`, `result` | Tool execution result |
   417|| `message_complete` | `message` | Full assistant message |
   418|| `usage` | `usage`, `model` | Token usage for a single model call |
   419|| `cost_update` | `summary` | Updated cost summary after each model call |
   420|| `turn_complete` | `usage`, `model`, `callCount` | Accumulated usage for the full agent turn |
   421|| `retry_attempt` | `attempt`, `maxRetries`, `delayMs`, `error` | A retryable error occurred; waiting before retry |
   422|| `retry_exhausted` | `attempts`, `error` | All retries exhausted |
   423|| `compact_start` | | Auto-compaction started |
   424|| `compact_complete` | | Auto-compaction finished |
   425|| `permission_request` | `toolName`, `input`, `message` | Tool call requires user approval |
   426|| `subagent_start` | `toolUseId`, `prompt` | A subagent is being spawned |
   427|| `subagent_end` | `toolUseId`, `result` | A subagent finished |
   428|| `session_resumed` | `sessionId`, `messageCount` | A previous session was restored |
   429|| `memory_update` | `created`, `updated`, `deleted` | Memories were extracted from the conversation |
   430|| `structured_output` | `output` | Structured output was produced |
   431|| `max_turns_reached` | `turns` | The agent hit the maxTurns limit |
   432|| `error` | `error` | An error occurred |
   433|
   434|See **[noumen.dev/docs/stream-events](https://noumen.dev/docs/stream-events)** for the full event reference including permissions, tracing, recovery, and more.
   435|
   436|## Built-in Tools
   437|
   438|### Core tools (always available)
   439|
   440|| Tool | Description |
   441||------|-------------|
   442|| **ReadFile** | Read files with line numbers, offset/limit support |
   443|| **WriteFile** | Create or overwrite files |
   444|| **EditFile** | Find-and-replace string editing |
   445|| **Bash** | Execute shell commands |
   446|| **Glob** | Find files by glob pattern (via ripgrep) |
   447|| **Grep** | Search file contents by regex (via ripgrep) |
   448|| **WebFetch** | Fetch a URL and return contents as markdown |
   449|| **NotebookEdit** | Edit Jupyter notebook cells (replace, insert, delete) |
   450|| **AskUser** | Ask the user a question and wait for a response |
   451|
   452|### Optional tools (enabled via Code options)
   453|
   454|| Tool | Requires | Description |
   455||------|----------|-------------|
   456|| **Agent** | `enableSubagents` | Spawn an isolated subagent for focused subtasks |
   457|| **Skill** | `skills` / `skillsPaths` | Invoke a named skill with arguments |
   458|| **TaskCreate** | `enableTasks` | Create a work item for tracking |
   459|| **TaskList** | `enableTasks` | List all tasks with status |
   460|| **TaskGet** | `enableTasks` | Get task details by ID |
   461|| **TaskUpdate** | `enableTasks` | Update task status/description |
   462|| **EnterPlanMode** | `enablePlanMode` | Switch to read-only exploration mode |
   463|| **ExitPlanMode** | `enablePlanMode` | Return to normal mode with optional plan |
   464|| **EnterWorktree** | `enableWorktrees` | Create an isolated git worktree |
   465|| **ExitWorktree** | `enableWorktrees` | Leave and optionally clean up worktree |
   466|| **LSP** | `enableLsp` | Query language servers (definitions, references, hover) |
   467|| **WebSearch** | `webSearch` config | Search the web via a user-provided backend |
   468|| **ToolSearch** | `toolSearch` | Discover deferred tools on demand (reduces context usage) |
   469|
   470|## Extended Thinking
   471|
   472|Enable model reasoning/thinking for supported providers. Each provider maps the config to its native format:
   473|
   474|- **Anthropic**: Sets `thinking.budget_tokens` on the API call
   475|- **OpenAI**: Maps to `reasoning_effort: "high"` for o-series models
   476|- **Gemini**: Sets `thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget`
   477|
   478|```typescript
   479|const code = new Code({
   480|  aiProvider,
   481|  sandbox,
   482|  options: {
   483|    thinking: { type: "enabled", budgetTokens: 10000 },
   484|  },
   485|});
   486|
   487|for await (const event of thread.run("Solve this complex problem")) {
   488|  if (event.type === "thinking_delta") {
   489|    process.stderr.write(event.text); // reasoning trace
   490|  }
   491|  if (event.type === "text_delta") {
   492|    process.stdout.write(event.text); // final answer
   493|  }
   494|}
   495|```
   496|
   497|Disable explicitly with `{ type: "disabled" }`, or omit the option entirely for default behavior.
   498|
   499|## Retry / Error Resilience
   500|
   501|Automatic retries with exponential backoff, Retry-After header support, context overflow recovery, and model fallback. Handles 429 (rate limit), 529 (overloaded), 500/502/503 (server errors), and connection failures.
   502|
   503|```typescript
   504|const code = new Code({
   505|  aiProvider,
   506|  sandbox,
   507|  options: {
   508|    retry: true, // use sensible defaults
   509|  },
   510|});
   511|
   512|// Or customize:
   513|const code2 = new Code({
   514|  aiProvider,
   515|  sandbox,
   516|  options: {
   517|    retry: {
   518|      maxRetries: 10,
   519|      baseDelayMs: 500,
   520|      maxDelayMs: 32000,
   521|      retryableStatuses: [408, 429, 500, 502, 503, 529],
   522|      fallbackModel: "gpt-4o-mini",     // switch model after repeated 529s
   523|      maxConsecutiveOverloaded: 3,
   524|      onRetry: (attempt, error, delayMs) => {
   525|        console.log(`Retry ${attempt}, waiting ${delayMs}ms: ${error.message}`);
   526|      },
   527|    },
   528|  },
   529|});
   530|```
   531|
   532|On context overflow (input + max_tokens > context limit), the engine automatically reduces `max_tokens` and retries — no manual intervention needed.
   533|
   534|## Cost Tracking
   535|
   536|Track token usage and estimate USD costs across all model calls. Includes built-in pricing for Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, and o-series models.
   537|
   538|```typescript
   539|const code = new Code({
   540|  aiProvider,
   541|  sandbox,
   542|  options: {
   543|    costTracking: { enabled: true },
   544|  },
   545|});
   546|
   547|const thread = code.createThread();
   548|
   549|for await (const event of thread.run("Refactor the auth module")) {
   550|  if (event.type === "cost_update") {
   551|    console.log(`Running cost: $${event.summary.totalCostUSD.toFixed(4)}`);
   552|  }
   553|}
   554|
   555|// Or get the summary at any time
   556|const summary = code.getCostSummary();
   557|console.log(`Total: $${summary.totalCostUSD.toFixed(4)}`);
   558|console.log(`Input tokens: ${summary.totalInputTokens}`);
   559|console.log(`Output tokens: ${summary.totalOutputTokens}`);
   560|```
   561|
   562|Supply custom pricing for unlisted models:
   563|
   564|```typescript
   565|const code = new Code({
   566|  aiProvider,
   567|  sandbox,
   568|  options: {
   569|    costTracking: {
   570|      enabled: true,
   571|      pricing: {
   572|        "my-custom-model": {
   573|          inputTokens: 1,    // USD per 1M tokens
   574|          outputTokens: 3,
   575|        },
   576|      },
   577|    },
   578|  },
   579|});
   580|```
   581|
   582|## Skills
   583|
   584|Skills are markdown instructions injected into the system prompt. Provide them inline or load from `SKILL.md` files on the virtual filesystem:
   585|
   586|```typescript
   587|const code = new Code({
   588|  aiProvider,
   589|  sandbox,
   590|  options: {
   591|    skills: [
   592|      { name: "Testing", content: "Always write vitest tests for new code." },
   593|    ],
   594|    skillsPaths: [".claude/skills", "~/.config/skills"],
   595|  },
   596|});
   597|
   598|// If using skillsPaths, call init() to pre-load them
   599|await code.init();
   600|```
   601|
   602|## Project Context (NOUMEN.md / CLAUDE.md)
   603|
   604|Drop a `NOUMEN.md` or `CLAUDE.md` in your project root to give the agent persistent instructions:
   605|
   606|```markdown
   607|# Project instructions
   608|
   609|This is a TypeScript monorepo. Use strict mode. Write vitest tests for all new code.
   610|```
   611|
   612|Enable it with `projectContext: true` in your `Code` options. The loader discovers context files from four layers — managed (enterprise), user (`~/.noumen/`), project (repo ancestors), and local (`.local.md`, gitignored) — so you can scope instructions at any level.
   613|
   614|This is fully compatible with `CLAUDE.md`. If your project already has one, noumen picks it up automatically. Both `NOUMEN.md` and `CLAUDE.md` can coexist in the same directory. The format supports `@path` includes, conditional rules via `paths:` frontmatter in `.noumen/rules/` directories, and hierarchical overriding.
   615|
   616|See **[noumen.dev/docs/context](https://noumen.dev/docs/context)** for full configuration options.
   617|
   618|## Sessions
   619|
   620|Conversations are persisted as JSONL files on the virtual filesystem. Each line is a serialized message entry. Compaction writes a boundary marker followed by a summary, so resumed sessions only load post-boundary messages.
   621|
   622|```typescript
   623|// List all saved sessions
   624|const sessions = await code.listSessions();
   625|// [{ sessionId, createdAt, lastMessageAt, title?, messageCount }]
   626|```
   627|
   628|## Hooks
   629|
   630|Intercept tool calls, turn lifecycle, subagent spawning, compaction, and errors:
   631|
   632|```typescript
   633|const code = new Code({
   634|  aiProvider, sandbox,
   635|  options: {
   636|    hooks: [
   637|      {
   638|        event: "PreToolUse",
   639|        matcher: "Bash",
   640|        handler: async (input) => {
   641|          console.log(`Bash: ${input.toolInput.command}`);
   642|          return { decision: "allow" };
   643|        },
   644|      },
   645|      {
   646|        event: "TurnEnd",
   647|        handler: async (input) => {
   648|          console.log(`Turn ended for ${input.sessionId}`);
   649|        },
   650|      },
   651|    ],
   652|  },
   653|});
   654|```
   655|
   656|Events: `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `TurnStart`, `TurnEnd`, `SubagentStart`, `SubagentStop`, `PreCompact`, `PostCompact`, `Error`.
   657|
   658|## Permissions
   659|
   660|Control what tools the agent can use with modes and rules:
   661|
   662|```typescript
   663|options: {
   664|  permissions: {
   665|    mode: "default", // or "plan", "acceptEdits", "auto", "bypassPermissions", "dontAsk"
   666|    rules: [
   667|      { toolName: "Bash", behavior: "ask", source: "project" },
   668|      { toolName: "ReadFile", behavior: "allow", source: "user" },
   669|    ],
   670|    handler: async (request) => ({ allow: true }),
   671|  },
   672|}
   673|```
   674|
   675|## Multi-Agent Swarm
   676|
   677|Run multiple agents in parallel with message passing:
   678|
   679|```typescript
   680|import { SwarmManager, InProcessBackend } from "noumen";
   681|
   682|const backend = new InProcessBackend(code);
   683|const swarm = new SwarmManager(backend, { maxConcurrent: 3 });
   684|
   685|await swarm.spawn({ name: "researcher", prompt: "Find all TODOs" });
   686|await swarm.spawn({ name: "writer", prompt: "Write tests for auth" });
   687|await swarm.waitForAll();
   688|```
   689|
   690|## Memory
   691|
   692|Persist knowledge across sessions:
   693|
   694|```typescript
   695|import { FileMemoryProvider, LocalFs } from "noumen";
   696|
   697|options: {
   698|  memory: {
   699|    provider: new FileMemoryProvider(new LocalFs({ basePath: ".noumen/memory" })),
   700|    autoExtract: true,
   701|    injectIntoSystemPrompt: true,
   702|  },
   703|}
   704|```
   705|
   706|## MCP (Model Context Protocol)
   707|
   708|Connect to MCP servers to discover and use external tools:
   709|
   710|```typescript
   711|options: {
   712|  mcpServers: {
   713|    filesystem: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/tmp"] },
   714|    remote: { type: "http", url: "http://localhost:3001/mcp" },
   715|  },
   716|}
   717|```
   718|
   719|Or expose noumen's tools as an MCP server (requires `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`):
   720|
   721|```bash
   722|pnpm add @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
   723|```
   724|
   725|```typescript
   726|import { createMcpServer } from "noumen/mcp";
   727|const server = createMcpServer({ tools: registry.listTools() });
   728|```
   729|
   730|## Tracing
   731|
   732|Instrument agent runs with OpenTelemetry:
   733|
   734|```typescript
   735|import { OTelTracer } from "noumen";
   736|
   737|options: {
   738|  tracing: { tracer: await OTelTracer.create("my-agent") },
   739|}
   740|```
   741|
   742|Falls back to no-op if `@opentelemetry/api` is not installed.
   743|
   744|## Full Documentation
   745|
   746|See **[noumen.dev](https://noumen.dev)** for complete documentation on all features including hooks, permissions, compaction strategies, LSP integration, task management, worktrees, plan mode, and more.
